skill.name: typescript-dev
skill.description: TypeScript development best practices, code quality tools, and documentation templates. Activated when working with .ts, .tsx files or TypeScript projects.
skill.summary: Guides TypeScript projects toward stricter types, cleaner documentation, and consistent quality checks.
skill.author: Cain96

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks and TypeScript examples as executable risk, but review found no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection. The dynamic require, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections are false positives from guidance text and code examples. The only minor concern is that the skill recommends local pnpm commands, which should run only in trusted projects.

low_findings.title: Local Project Commands Suggested
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with low severity: SKILL.md recommends running pnpm format, lint, type-check, and test commands. These are normal TypeScript workflow commands, but they execute project-defined scripts and should be used only in trusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and executable, but they are standard local development commands. No command interpolation, secret access, or remote payload execution is present.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Require Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The detected require references appear in guidance that tells users to avoid require and use ES modules. This is safer TypeScript advice, not dynamic module loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are prose checklist items that discourage require usage. There is no executable require call or variable-controlled import.

low_findings.title: Network Example Is Same-Origin Demonstration
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The fetch call is inside a TypeScript async example and targets a relative application route. No external domain, credential transfer, or exfiltration behavior is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network pattern is present only in documentation example code. The URL is relative, and the surrounding text presents ordinary async error handling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance was found. The cited content is TypeScript guidance, React performance advice, or example syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation, host inspection, environment probing, or system enumeration. The static matches appear to come from benign words and code examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project Script Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes pnpm commands for formatting, linting, type checking, and tests. This is expected for a TypeScript development skill, but project scripts can execute arbitrary code if the repository is untrusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and could trigger local package scripts. The risk is contextual because the commands themselves are standard and contain no suspicious arguments.

content.value_statement: TypeScript projects often drift into weak types, inconsistent checks, and unclear public APIs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for stricter, cleaner TypeScript work.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of strict TypeScript settings and safer null handling.
Recommends ES modules and discourages require usage.
Provides a quality workflow for formatting, linting, type checking, and coverage tests.
Defines review checklist items for type safety, error handling, and documentation.
Shows patterns for typed errors, Result objects, async handling, and type guards.
Encourages TSDoc for exported APIs when the intent is not clear.
limitations: It does not include executable helper scripts or automated fixes.
It assumes pnpm is the package manager for the project.
It gives general TypeScript guidance rather than framework-specific rules.
It does not verify test coverage or TypeScript settings by itself.

use_cases.title: Review TypeScript Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to evaluate type safety, public API documentation, error handling, and test coverage before merging.

use_cases.title: Set Project Quality Standards
use_cases.description: Apply the recommended pnpm workflow and strict TypeScript rules when standardizing a repository.

use_cases.title: Improve Everyday TypeScript Code
use_cases.description: Ask for safer types, clearer generics, better null handling, and stronger type guards while editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Check a File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one TypeScript file
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TypeScript file using the typescript-dev checklist. Focus on strict types, null handling, and avoid any usage.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Public APIs
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these exported functions and interfaces. Suggest TSDoc only where the signature does not make the intent clear.

prompt_templates.title: Harden Error Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Refactor service or data access logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this TypeScript module for error handling. Recommend typed errors, Result-style returns, and safer async handling where useful.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Repository Standards
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this TypeScript project for strict configuration, pnpm workflow alignment, type-only imports, test coverage, and recurring anti-patterns.

output_examples.input: Review a TypeScript service that uses any, unchecked API responses, and unclear exported functions.
output: Flags any usage and recommends explicit domain types.
Suggests a type guard for unknown API response data.
Identifies exported functions that need short TSDoc comments.
Recommends running format, lint, type check, and coverage tests.

output_examples.input: Assess a React TypeScript component with expensive derived values and broad prop types.
output: Suggests narrower prop unions where appropriate.
Calls out memoization only for expensive repeated calculations.
Recommends type-only imports for shared interfaces.
Notes tests needed for important rendering states.

best_practices: Run the recommended quality commands after meaningful code changes.
Use strict TypeScript settings and prefer unknown plus type guards over any.
Document exported APIs only when names and signatures do not explain intent.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to justify broad any types in production code.
Do not run project scripts from repositories you do not trust.
Do not add excessive comments when clearer names or types would solve the issue.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, review criteria, and patterns for TypeScript development.

faq.question: Which package manager does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects pnpm and recommends pnpm commands for quality checks.

faq.question: Can it help with React TypeScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes React performance guidance, but it is not a full React framework guide.

faq.question: Does it require TSDoc everywhere?
faq.answer: No. It recommends TSDoc for public APIs when the signature does not make the intent clear.

faq.question: Is network access required?
faq.answer: No. The only fetch reference is a same-origin example used to explain async error handling.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no malicious behavior, but users should run local project scripts only in trusted repositories.
